title: Algal nuclei isolation for Nanopore sequencing of HMW DNA
author: Robert Auber
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7b7hirn

steps.title: Chill centrifuge to 4 °C
steps.instruction: Chill centrifuge to 4 °C

steps.title: Chill 50 ml conical tubes (4...
steps.instruction: Chill 50 ml conical tubes (4 per sample) and NIB buffers on ice.

steps.title: Transfer 20 mL to 50 mL...
steps.instruction: Transfer 20 mL to 50 mL of culture to a pre-chilled 50 ml conical tube and centrifuge at 4 °C for 00:10:00 to pellet cells. The amount of culture spun down as well as the centrifugation speed at which cultures are pelleted should be optimized for each organism. In our experience, the pellet size should be at least the size of a lentil for small (5 μm) for our alga.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and snap freeze...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and snap freeze the conical tube in liquid nitrogen for at least 00:03:00 . Some organisms will require some type of tissue disruption to lyse cells.

steps.title: Place cell pellet on ice and...
steps.instruction: Place cell pellet on ice and add 10 mL of ice cold NIB. If you know your pellet is not very soluble, only add 1mL of NIB at first and mix by pipetting up and down with a 1mL pipette. Add remaining 9mL NIB after clumps are broken up.

steps.title: Mix by slowly pipetting up and...
steps.instruction: Mix by slowly pipetting up and down with a 10mL serological pipettor until the mixture is smooth and not clumpy.

steps.title: Place a 100 μm filter on...
steps.instruction: Place a 100 μm filter on top of a pre-chilled 50 ml conical tube.

steps.title: Slowly pour the sample through the...
steps.instruction: Slowly pour the sample through the filter into the clean tube.

steps.title: Use a serological pipettor to wash...
steps.instruction: Use a serological pipettor to wash the filter with 10 mL of ice cold NIB.

steps.title: Place a 70 μm filter on...
steps.instruction: Place a 70 μm filter on top of a second pre-chilled 50 ml conical tube.

steps.title: Slowly pour the sample through the...
steps.instruction: Slowly pour the sample through the filter into the clean tube.

steps.title: Use a serological pipettor to wash...
steps.instruction: Use a serological pipettor to wash the filter with 10 mL of ice cold NIB.

steps.title: Place a 40 μm filter on...
steps.instruction: Place a 40 μm filter on top of a third pre-chilled 50 ml conical tube.

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.5 mL of ice cold NIB + TritonX solution to filtrate.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4 °C for 00:10:00 to pellet nuclei
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4 °C for 00:10:00 to pellet nuclei. The speed at which the sample is spun down should be reevaluated for different organims based on genome size.

steps.title: Aliquot 1 µL of the pellet into a clean 1
steps.instruction: Aliquot 1 µL of the pellet into a clean 1.5ml eppendorf tube. Add 18 µL of fresh NIB and 1 µL of propidium iodide to the tube.

steps.title: Extract DNA by referring to Circulomics...
steps.instruction: Extract DNA by referring to Circulomics Plant Nuclei Big DNA Kit protocol. https://www.circulomics.com/store/Nanobind-Plant-Nuclei-Big-DNA-Kit-Alpha-Version-p99924200

steps.title: Slowly pour the sample through the...
steps.instruction: Slowly pour the sample through the filter into the clean tube. (Do not wash with NIB)

steps.title: Transfer the stained nuclei to a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the stained nuclei to a microscope and confirm presence of fluorescing nuclei under an epifluorescent light. Image was taken on a Nikon Ts2R-FL microscope with a CFP LP cube (Excitation 448/23nm (436.5-459.5nm), Emission 472nm Long Pass)

steps.title: Discard the supernatant
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Add BME to premade NIB
steps.instruction: Add BME to premade NIB. Per sample add 35 µL BME into 35 mL premade NIB.

steps.title: Set thermomixer to 55 °C
steps.instruction: Set thermomixer to 55 °C

steps.title: Cool solutions to 0 °C on ice
steps.instruction: Cool solutions to 0 °C on ice.

steps.title: Make 10% Triton X-100 NIB solution
steps.instruction: Make 10% Triton X-100 NIB solution. Per sample, aliquot 1.8 mL NIB prepared in previous step and add 200 µL Triton X-100.
